terms.definition: A framework that integrates formal control theory with LLM-based agents to provide mathematical guarantees for autonomous behavior in high-stakes environments. It ensures that even when an agent encounters adversarial pressure or unexpected data, its actions remain within predefined safety and operational boundaries.
terms.analogy: It is like an Electronic Stability Control (ESC) system in a high-performance car; while the driver (or AI) handles the steering, the system monitors wheel sensors to automatically apply brakes and prevent the vehicle from spinning out of control during a sharp turn.
terms.business_value: Shifts AI from 'best-effort' experimental pilots to 'mission-critical' deployments by mitigating the liability of unpredictable autonomous decisions in cybersecurity, finance, and industrial operations.

terms.definition: The ability of an AI agent to identify latent physical or functional properties of an object—beyond its primary intended use—to solve a novel problem. This involves mapping abstract goals to the physical characteristics of available tools, such as using a heavy paperweight as a hammer when a traditional hammer is missing.
terms.analogy: Imagine a chef who discovers the kitchen's immersion blender is broken and creatively uses a cordless power drill with a whisk attachment to finish the sauce; the agent identifies the 'rotation' affordance of the drill to meet the 'mixing' goal.
terms.business_value: Enables extreme operational agility; systems with this capability can troubleshoot supply chain or hardware failures by finding creative workarounds with existing inventory, drastically reducing downtime when specific resources are unavailable.

terms.definition: A machine learning technique that identifies the underlying mathematical equations—rather than just numerical patterns—that best describe a given dataset. Unlike 'black-box' neural networks, it produces human-readable formulas that offer transparent insights into the physical or economic laws governing a system's behavior.
terms.analogy: Instead of a car's GPS just telling you that you will arrive in 20 minutes (prediction), symbolic regression provides you with the exact physics formula including speed, wind resistance, and fuel consumption that explains why it takes 20 minutes (explanation).
terms.business_value: Transforms data into intellectual property; by uncovering the literal 'laws' of your market or manufacturing process, you gain transparent, auditable models that are easier to defend to regulators and more robust for long-term strategic forecasting.
